title: Mollinedia umbellata
taxonomy: Mollinedia umbellata belongs to the family Hernandiaceae, which is a family of flowering plants native to tropical regions. This family is part of the order Laurales, which comprises flowering plants that are often woody and aromatic. The order Laurales is further grouped under the clade Magnoliids, which is a large clade of flowering plants that diverged from other flowering plants early in evolutionary history. The Magnoliids clade is part of the larger clade Angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. The classification of Mollinedia umbellata reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
